refined_fr.refined_fr: Capture and represent worksite spatial features in a machine-readable model.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR conflates three distinct functional obligations: (1) terrain analysis, (2) obstacle identification, and (3) work-zone representation. Each is a separate concern with different data sources, processing logic, and stakeholder consumption patterns. The refined FR is solution-neutral (avoids 'mapping/modeling' terminology which implies implementation choice) and focuses on the core problem: making spatial features available in a structured, reusable form. Atomicity is achieved by deferring the specific feature types (terrain, obstacles, zones) to sub-FRs.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Verify that a worksite model exists in a defined schema/format, contains spatial coordinates and feature classifications, and is accessible to downstream planning and execution functions per the acceptance criteria.
sub_frs: FR-NGHE-006.1: Acquire terrain elevation and surface properties across the worksite.
FR-NGHE-006.2: Identify and locate static obstacles (structures, vegetation, utilities) within the worksite boundary.
FR-NGHE-006.3: Define and represent designated work zones (operational areas, restricted zones, safety perimeters) in the worksite model.
FR-NGHE-006.4: Persist and version the worksite model for access by planning and execution functions.

candidate_dps: DP1: Acquire spatial feature data through multi-modal sensing (vision, range, inertial) and fuse into a unified coordinate frame; store as a structured spatial graph with feature attributes (geometry, classification, safety constraints); expose via query interface for downstream planning/execution consumers.

DP2: Decompose worksite into discrete zones; assign zone-level feature inventories (obstacles, hazards, access points) via manual annotation or semi-automated detection; maintain zone-feature mappings in a relational schema; provide zone-based query and update APIs.

DP3: Capture worksite features as a voxel-grid occupancy model with semantic labels; support incremental updates from streaming sensor data; provide spatial query (collision, proximity, traversability) and export to planning-compatible formats.

DP4: Integrate external worksite blueprints/CAD models as reference geometry; overlay real-time sensor observations to detect deviations and dynamic features; maintain a hybrid static-dynamic feature registry; reconcile conflicts via confidence scoring.

DP5: Establish a feature capture pipeline: raw sensor ingestion → feature extraction (edge or centralized) → validation against safety/operational policy rules → persistent storage in a versioned feature repository; expose immutable snapshots and delta feeds to consumers.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP5: Establish a feature capture pipeline: raw sensor ingestion → feature extraction (edge or centralized) → validation against safety/operational policy rules → persistent storage in a versioned feature repository; expose immutable snapshots and delta feeds to consumers.

potential_sources: Feature extraction logic tightly coupled to specific sensor modalities (risk: DP becomes technology-specific; mitigation: abstract sensor input as normalized observations).
Validation rules hardcoded to safety/operational policies (risk: policy changes require code changes; mitigation: externalize rules as declarative policy configurations).

Snapshot/delta feed interface design must align with downstream planning/execution consumer expectations (risk: interface mismatch; mitigation: define consumer contracts early via acceptance criteria).

Versioning and conflict resolution strategy not yet specified (risk: unclear how concurrent updates or sensor disagreements are handled; mitigation: define versioning semantics and reconciliation rules in IM layer).
coupling_check.notes: DP5 is selected because it explicitly separates concerns (ingestion, extraction, validation, storage, exposure) and aligns with the acceptance criterion that a worksite model is 'generated and available.' The pipeline structure supports policy-driven validation and enables traceability. Coupling risks are manageable through abstraction layers and externalized configuration.

assumptions_risks: Assumption: Worksite safety plan and operational policies are available in machine-readable form or can be translated to rules. Risk: If policies are informal or ambiguous, validation step may fail or require manual intervention. Mitigation: Establish policy formalization as a prerequisite.

Assumption: Downstream planning/execution functions can consume immutable snapshots and delta feeds. Risk: If consumers expect real-time streaming or require different granularity, interface mismatch occurs. Mitigation: Validate consumer contracts during IM design.

Assumption: Feature extraction (edge or centralized) can achieve sufficient accuracy and latency for planning/execution. Risk: Poor extraction quality or high latency degrades downstream decision-making. Mitigation: Define extraction SLOs (accuracy, latency) and validate against acceptance criteria.

Assumption: Sensor data is sufficiently rich to capture all agreed-upon site features. Risk: Blind spots, occlusions, or feature types outside sensor capability. Mitigation: Establish sensor coverage requirements and fallback mechanisms (e.g., manual annotation).

Assumption: Versioning and conflict resolution strategy is defined. Risk: Concurrent updates or sensor disagreements cause data inconsistency. Mitigation: Define versioning semantics (e.g., last-write-wins, consensus, manual review) in IM layer.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Refine DP5 to explicitly address: (1) Sensor abstraction layer to decouple feature extraction from specific modalities; (2) Policy rule engine interface to externalize safety/operational validation; (3) Consumer contract specification (snapshot schema, delta format, query API) to ensure downstream compatibility; (4) Versioning and reconciliation strategy (e.g., immutable append-only log with conflict resolution rules); (5) Acceptance criteria mapping: confirm that 'worksite model/map generated and available' is satisfied by the snapshot/delta feed interface and that all agreed site features are captured by the extraction pipeline. Consider introducing a secondary FR: 'Validate captured features against safety/operational policies' to make policy enforcement explicit and traceable.

im_boundary.name: Safety Services
im_boundary.scope: Centralized feature capture pipeline including raw sensor ingestion, feature extraction, validation against safety/operational policies, persistent versioned storage, and exposure of immutable snapshots and delta feeds to authorized consumers.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Raw sensor data ingestion from Vehicle and Teleoperation subsystems; multi-modal streams (LiDAR, camera, IMU, GPS, etc.)

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Validation results and policy violation alerts; immutable feature snapshots and delta feeds for downstream safety decision-making

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Persistent storage backend communication for versioned feature repository; potential edge-to-cloud synchronization for distributed extraction

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Policy rule updates and feature extraction configuration directives from NGHE Solution or operator

module_containment_scores.notes: Excellent fit: Safety Services is the natural owner of policy validation, safety-critical feature curation, and immutable audit trails. Feature extraction logic (edge or centralized) aligns with safety-critical processing. Minimal diffusion: only Telemetry ingestion (in), SafetyAlert output (out), and Network Link for storage are external. Versioned repository and snapshot/delta feed exposure are core safety service responsibilities.

Poor fit: Vehicle is a data source, not a processing/validation hub. Feature extraction, policy validation, and persistent versioned storage belong upstream. High diffusion: pipeline logic would leak across Vehicle, Teleoperation, and NGHE Solution. Violates separation of concerns.

Poor fit: Teleoperation is a control/command interface, not a feature processing or safety validation layer. Feature capture and policy enforcement are orthogonal to teleoperation concerns. High diffusion: would require duplication of validation logic and storage management across multiple subsystems.

Moderate-to-poor fit: NGHE Solution is a high-level orchestrator/planner, not a real-time feature pipeline operator. Placing feature capture, extraction, and validation here creates a monolithic, tightly-coupled architecture. Diffusion risk: feature repository and delta feeds would need to be exposed back to Vehicle and Teleoperation, creating circular dependencies. Better as a consumer of Safety Services outputs.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Safety Services is the single authoritative owner of the feature capture pipeline because: (1) policy validation is inherently a safety function; (2) immutable versioned storage and audit trails are safety-critical requirements; (3) feature snapshots and delta feeds are safety decision inputs; (4) centralized extraction (with optional edge preprocessing) aligns with safety-critical processing patterns; (5) all external interfaces (Telemetry in, SafetyAlert out, Network Link for storage, Tasking for policy updates) are natural to Safety Services' role.
containment_risks: If edge feature extraction is required on Vehicle or Teleoperation subsystems, a portion of the extraction logic may need to diffuse to those modules; mitigate by defining a thin, stateless edge extraction interface (part of Telemetry contract) that feeds into Safety Services' centralized validation and storage.

If NGHE Solution requires real-time feature access for planning, SafetyAlert interface may become bidirectional or require a separate feature query interface; mitigate by designing SafetyAlert as a publish-subscribe feed rather than request-response.

If multiple independent feature repositories are needed (e.g., one for safety, one for analytics), the DP may need revision to clarify single-source-of-truth ownership; current DP assumes one versioned repository.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: No revision to DP required. Safety Services is a sufficiently specific and cohesive boundary. If edge extraction is mandatory, refine DP to: 'Establish a feature capture pipeline: raw sensor ingestion → lightweight edge feature extraction (stateless, Vehicle/Teleoperation-resident) → centralized feature validation against safety/operational policy rules in Safety Services → persistent storage in a versioned feature repository → expose immutable snapshots and delta feeds to consumers.' This clarifies that edge extraction is a preprocessing step, not a full pipeline, and Safety Services owns validation and storage.

fr_dp_gate.reason: DP5 directly addresses FR by specifying a complete pipeline (ingestion → extraction → validation → storage → exposure) that captures and represents worksite spatial features in machine-readable form. The pipeline explicitly includes validation against safety/operational policies and persistent versioned storage, ensuring features are both captured and made available to downstream consumers (planning/execution functions). The DP is sufficiently detailed to be implementable and traceable to acceptance criteria.

diffusion_risks: Edge feature extraction logic may diffuse to Vehicle or Teleoperation subsystems if real-time preprocessing is required; mitigate by defining a thin, stateless edge extraction interface as part of the Telemetry ingestion contract, with Safety Services owning centralized validation and storage.

If NGHE Solution requires synchronous feature queries for planning (beyond asynchronous delta feeds), SafetyAlert interface may become bidirectional or require a separate query interface; mitigate by designing SafetyAlert as a publish-subscribe feed and providing a separate read-only feature snapshot query interface if needed.

Multiple independent feature repositories (safety vs. analytics) could fragment ownership; current DP assumes single versioned repository. If separation is required, clarify which module owns each repository and how they synchronize.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Safety Services is the optimal IM boundary with 0.92 containment score and 0.08 diffusion score. All core responsibilities (policy validation, immutable audit trails, versioned storage, snapshot/delta feed exposure) are naturally aligned with safety-critical processing. External interfaces (Telemetry in, SafetyAlert out, Network Link for storage, Tasking for policy updates) are clean and well-scoped. No alternative module (Vehicle, Teleoperation, NGHE Solution) provides better cohesion or lower diffusion. Ownership is unambiguous.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: High. Acceptance criteria require that 'a worksite model/map is generated and available to planning/execution functions for an agreed set of site features.' Safety Services' immutable snapshots and delta feeds directly satisfy this requirement. Versioned storage enables traceability and audit compliance. Feature extraction and validation logic can be unit-tested against safety/operational policies. Integration tests can verify that Telemetry ingestion, SafetyAlert output, and feature repository queries function correctly.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear. FR is allocated entirely to Safety Services IM boundary. Telemetry subsystem provides raw sensor data (input interface); Vehicle and Teleoperation provide sensor streams (data sources, not processing owners); NGHE Solution and other consumers receive feature snapshots and delta feeds (output interface). No ambiguity in responsibility allocation. Worksite safety plan and operational policies are inputs to the Tasking interface (policy rule updates), ensuring alignment with constraints.

recommended_revisions: Refine DP5 to explicitly distinguish edge preprocessing from centralized validation: 'Establish a feature capture pipeline: raw sensor ingestion → lightweight edge feature extraction (stateless, Vehicle/Teleoperation-resident, optional) → centralized feature validation against safety/operational policy rules in Safety Services → persistent storage in a versioned feature repository → expose immutable snapshots and delta feeds to authorized consumers.' This clarifies that Safety Services owns validation and storage, reducing diffusion risk if edge extraction is implemented.

Define the Tasking interface contract explicitly: specify the schema for policy rule updates, feature extraction configuration directives, and versioning/rollback semantics. This ensures NGHE Solution and operators can safely update policies without corrupting the feature repository.

Specify the SafetyAlert interface as a publish-subscribe feed (not request-response) to support asynchronous consumption by multiple downstream consumers (planning, execution, monitoring) without creating circular dependencies.

Document the single-source-of-truth assumption: confirm that one versioned feature repository is authoritative. If multiple repositories are needed (e.g., safety vs. analytics), clarify ownership and synchronization strategy in a separate DP revision.

decision.justification: All three gates (FR-DP, DP-IM, FR-IM) pass with high confidence and clear ownership allocation to Safety Services. However, the gate results explicitly identify four critical diffusion risks and architectural clarifications that require decomposition into sub-level FRs to resolve. The recommended revisions indicate that FR5 is sufficiently complex to warrant functional decomposition: (1) edge preprocessing vs. centralized validation requires separate FR treatment; (2) policy rule ingestion and versioning is a distinct functional concern; (3) feature exposure (snapshots vs. delta feeds) requires separate interface design; (4) repository synchronization (if multi-repo) is a separate concern. Given high safety criticality, decomposing these concerns into explicit sub-FRs ensures each is independently validated, traced, and owned. Stopping here would leave architectural ambiguity at implementation time; decomposition hardens the design.
next_level_sub_frs: FR5.1: Ingest and normalize raw sensor data (Telemetry streams) into a standardized feature representation format, with optional lightweight edge preprocessing to reduce data volume before centralized processing.
FR5.2: Validate extracted worksite features against safety-critical policies and operational constraints, maintaining an immutable audit trail of all validation decisions and policy rule versions.
FR5.3: Persist validated worksite features in a versioned, single-source-of-truth repository with rollback and historical query capabilities to support traceability and compliance audits.

FR5.4: Expose worksite features to authorized downstream consumers (planning, execution, monitoring) via immutable snapshots and asynchronous delta feeds, with explicit versioning and timestamp semantics.

FR5.5: Accept and apply policy rule updates (safety constraints, feature extraction configuration, validation thresholds) from authorized sources (NGHE Solution, operators) with version control and rollback semantics, ensuring no corruption of the feature repository.

revise.why: DP5 is logically sound but requires explicit refinement to address the four recommended revisions: (1) clarify the boundary between edge preprocessing (stateless, Vehicle/Teleoperation-resident, optional) and centralized validation (Safety Services-owned, policy-enforced); (2) define the Tasking interface contract schema for policy rule updates and versioning; (3) specify SafetyAlert as publish-subscribe (not request-response) to prevent circular dependencies; (4) document the single-source-of-truth assumption and clarify multi-repo synchronization if needed. These refinements should be incorporated into DP5 before sub-level DPs are derived, ensuring consistency and reducing rework.

assumptions: Safety Services is the optimal and unambiguous IM boundary for FR5 (containment score 0.92, diffusion score 0.08).

A single versioned feature repository is authoritative; if multiple repositories are required (safety vs. analytics), ownership and synchronization strategy will be clarified in a separate design decision.
Edge feature extraction (if implemented) is stateless and Vehicle/Teleoperation-resident; Safety Services owns centralized validation and storage only.
SafetyAlert interface is asynchronous (publish-subscribe), not synchronous request-response, to support multiple independent consumers without circular dependencies.
Tasking interface provides policy rule updates and feature extraction configuration directives; NGHE Solution and operators are authorized sources.
Acceptance criteria ('worksite model/map generated and available to planning/execution functions') are satisfied by immutable snapshots and delta feeds from Safety Services.
open_risks: Edge feature extraction logic may diffuse to Vehicle or Teleoperation subsystems if real-time preprocessing is required; mitigation: define a thin, stateless edge extraction interface as part of the Telemetry ingestion contract (FR5.1 sub-DP).

NGHE Solution may require synchronous feature queries for planning (beyond asynchronous delta feeds), creating bidirectional SafetyAlert interface or circular dependency; mitigation: design SafetyAlert as publish-subscribe feed and provide a separate read-only feature snapshot query interface (FR5.4 sub-DP).

Multiple independent feature repositories (safety vs. analytics) could fragment ownership and synchronization; mitigation: clarify single-source-of-truth assumption and document multi-repo strategy if separation is required (FR5.3 sub-DP).
Policy rule update schema and versioning semantics are not yet defined; mitigation: specify Tasking interface contract explicitly in FR5.5 sub-DP.
evidence_needed: Detailed Tasking interface contract: schema for policy rule updates, feature extraction configuration directives, versioning/rollback semantics, and authorization model.
SafetyAlert interface specification: publish-subscribe topic structure, message schema, versioning, timestamp semantics, and consumer subscription model.
Edge preprocessing specification (if required): stateless feature extraction logic, data volume reduction targets, latency constraints, and interface contract with Telemetry ingestion.
Feature repository schema: versioning strategy, rollback semantics, query interface (snapshots vs. delta feeds), and audit trail structure.
Multi-repository synchronization strategy (if required): ownership boundaries, consistency model, and conflict resolution for safety vs. analytics repositories.
Acceptance test cases: verify that worksite features are captured, validated, stored, and exposed to planning/execution functions with correct versioning and audit trails.
